messages.content:
CVE-2024-46981 is a critical vulnerability in Redis, an open-source, in-memory database. This vulnerability allows an authenticated user to execute remote code by exploiting the garbage collector through a specially crafted Lua script. The issue is resolved in Redis versions 7.4.2, 7.2.7, and 6.2.17.

The vulnerability is categorized under CWE-416, which involves referencing memory after it has been freed, leading to potential crashes or execution of unintended code. The severity is deemed critical due to the potential impact of remote code execution.

Vulnerable versions of Redis include 6.2.0 up to 6.2.17, 7.2.0 up to 7.2.7, and 7.4.0 up to 7.4.2, along with Debian Linux 11.0. Mitigation involves restricting the execution of Lua scripts using ACL settings to block EVAL and EVALSHA commands.

Associated with MITRE ATT\u0026CK technique T1190, this vulnerability underscores the importance of maintaining up-to-date software and employing access control measures to mitigate exploitation risks.
